question: The chart shows average website sign-ups per year, including the most recent partial year. Which statement is best supported by the data shown?

options.A: The latest year proves that sign-ups are now permanently declining.
options.B: The latest year clearly has the lowest sign-ups of the entire period.
options.C: None of the above conclusions about the full-year trend are justified from this partial-year data.
options.D: The latest year shows a sharp decline compared to previous years.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 5 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (1,205 sign-ups) directly to full-year values like 2,930 sign-ups creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
